facts.text: Los Angeles was founded on September 4, 1781, when 44 settlers called Los Pobladores established the pueblo El Pueblo de Nuestra Señora la Reina de los Ángeles.
One in every six Los Angeles residents works in a creative industry, making the city the world's creative capital.
Los Angeles hosted the Summer Olympics in 1932 and 1984 and will host again in 2028, becoming the third city to hold the Games three times.
Los Angeles is one of six North American cities to win championships in all five major leagues, with teams like the Dodgers, Lakers, and Kings.
The historic Tongva settlement of Yaanga, meaning "place of the poison oak," became the site where the Spanish founded Los Angeles.
